mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate, indicative of restrictive monetary policy, increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and raises the opportunity cost of holding equities versus safer assets like bonds, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the FEDFUNDS rate is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates strong underlying economic momentum. This translates into higher expected corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer demand (retail sales) is strong while inflation is also elevated, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening from the central bank to cool an overheating economy. This leads to a repricing of equity risk and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if, during co-occurring periods of high retail sales growth and high inflation, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime, a very low unemployment rate signals a tight labor market, which fuels wage-price spirals and forces the central bank to maintain a restrictive policy stance, negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: The conditional correlation between UNRATE level and 20-day forward returns is positive when the year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL is above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production is a direct measure of output growth for the economy's supply side. This strength translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive equity market returns over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a rolling 36-month correlation with 60-day forward returns that is consistently less than 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts indicates a significant slowdown in a key cyclical sector of the economy, signaling falling consumer confidence and investment, which precedes broader economic weakness and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in over 50% of the 60-day periods following a trigger event where housing starts fall more than 20% in 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of consumer price inflation erode the real value of future earnings and increase the likelihood of restrictive central bank policy, both of which exert downward pressure on equity prices over short horizons.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline real GDP growth remains strong but a key leading sector like housing shows significant deceleration, it signals a cyclical peak. Markets price in the impending broader slowdown, leading to negative short-term forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 0.5% and HOUST MoM growth < -5%
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 for 5 days upon a trigger fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a 5-year walk-forward test.
